summary.allowed_next_work: design review, manifest work, golden vectors, and tiny deterministic proof harnesses only
summary.forbidden_next_work: No broad seed search, no long-span sweep, no format promotion, no public preset registry, and no production acceleration claim.
summary.conclusion: The next viable research move is not compute; it is to choose and specify one materially new byte-to-seed mechanism with controls strong enough to reopen generated gates.

designs.title: Frozen rank-coded span generator
designs.whitepaper_link: table lookup from a seed table, but rank-coded and external-provenance first
designs.core_idea: Use seed bytes to index a frozen external span model: phrase ranks, byte-pair paths, and n-gram continuation ranks trained only from public external corpora before any Telomere held-out scan.
designs.why_materially_new: It attacks cryptographic-uniformity directly by making seed expansion rank-coded and corpus-shaped while preserving decoder-public reproducibility.
designs.implementation_slice: Create an external-source manifest, freeze rank tables/checksums, and run a tiny no-leakage replay only after provenance is complete.
designs.falsification_test: Held-out gains are explainable by equivalent random-trial scaling, flat dictionary lookup, or wrong-family/shadow controls.
designs.promotion_trigger: At least three unrelated ordinary held-out groups produce selected exact spans and full-stream negative rows after selector/version metadata, while paired shadow, random, wrong-family, binary, and high-entropy controls stay non-negative.
designs.stop_rule: Stop if any control goes negative, if ordinary wins stay below three groups, or if the rank table cannot be frozen from external provenance.
designs.compute_budget: manifest-only now; later bounded replay only, no raw-depth escalation

designs.title: Reversible boundary/chimera span frontier
designs.whitepaper_link: stronger frontier generator before exact seed-span search
designs.core_idea: Replace fixed/phase span candidates with reversible span proposals from grammar delimiters, rolling-min content-defined boundaries, repeated anchors, and safe chimera combinations of adjacent structural regions.
designs.why_materially_new: It changes which spans become eligible before exact matching, measuring frontier lift per scanned span instead of spending broader raw depth.
designs.implementation_slice: Implement a deterministic boundary manifest and compare fixed-phase, random-boundary, delimiter-shuffled, and content-defined frontiers on the same shallow generator budget.
control_suite: random boundary schedule with same length histogram
designs.falsification_test: Held-out max prefix stays at 4, selected spans stay zero, or boundary controls produce comparable prefix/exact density.
designs.promotion_trigger: With the same shallow generator budget, held-out ordinary corpora reach prefix>=5 or exact selected spans that fixed-phase and random-boundary controls do not produce.
designs.stop_rule: Stop if frontier lift is not better than random-boundary controls or requires raw-depth escalation.
designs.compute_budget: design and manifest first; shallow replay only after controls are frozen

designs.title: Decoder-public grammar expander
designs.whitepaper_link: Lotus preset / seed table, but generator-first instead of table-first
designs.core_idea: Replace uniform hash expansion with a versioned public generator whose seed space emits grammar productions, record delimiters, and common byte motifs for a declared family. The file stores only seed-span records plus the public preset/version.
designs.why_materially_new: It changes the seed-to-byte distribution itself instead of searching deeper through BLAKE3/SHA-256 or replaying fixed observed spans.
designs.implementation_slice: Implement a tiny decoder-public generator for one grammar family, a v2 experimental preset id, and a replay-only probe over held-out and shadow corpora.
designs.falsification_test: Header/token wins vanish after project-token scrubbing or paired shadow corpora shrink comparably.
designs.promotion_trigger: At least three unrelated ordinary held-out groups produce selected exact spans and full-stream negative delta after preset metadata; all shadow/binary/wrong-family controls remain non-negative.
designs.stop_rule: Stop if wins are dominated by project names, headers, fixed labels, or if the generator behaves like a static dictionary with the same control failures already observed.
designs.compute_budget: design-only now; later probe capped to frozen held-out matrix, no raw-depth escalation

designs.title: Typed record frontier generator
designs.whitepaper_link: stronger reversible frontier before exact seed-span search
designs.core_idea: Convert structured records into a canonical typed stream where seed spans target field tags, separators, lengths, and low-entropy value channels separately, then recompose bytes exactly.
designs.why_materially_new: It attempts to move natural data from prefix-4 steering evidence to prefix>=6 or selected exact spans by changing the reversible frontier, not by adding raw depth.
designs.implementation_slice: Prototype a decode-verified typed-record normalizer for JSONL/CSV/log fixtures with explicit metadata accounting and wrong-schema controls.
designs.falsification_test: Any negative delta disappears after field names are shadowed or when wrong-schema controls select comparable spans.
designs.promotion_trigger: Held-out ordinary rows reach prefix>=6 or selected exact spans after all transform metadata, with controls and wrong-schema rows null.
designs.stop_rule: Do not add transform metadata to .tlmr if the frontier only moves prefix-4 near misses or requires per-file schema training.
designs.compute_budget: manifest and tiny deterministic fixtures first; no depth-3/depth-4 escalation

designs.title: Motif-biased PRNG expander family
designs.whitepaper_link: search farther by arity, but through a non-uniform public expander
designs.core_idea: Define a small family of deterministic non-cryptographic expanders whose seeds emit byte motifs, delimiters, counters, and repeated micro-structures before falling back to uniform bytes.
designs.why_materially_new: It tests whether a decoder-public generative prior can beat the equivalent random-trial multiplier without storing a file-local table.
designs.implementation_slice: Implement three frozen motif expanders with preset ids and compare against an equal-number-of-draws SHA-256 baseline on held-out corpora.
designs.falsification_test: Selected spans scale no better than the random-trial multiplier or controls select comparable motifs.
designs.promotion_trigger: The motif family produces full-stream negative rows in at least three ordinary groups, zero control negative groups, and beats equivalent random draws.
designs.stop_rule: Stop if gains appear only from extra trials, fixed headers, or project-specific strings.
designs.compute_budget: predeclared motif set only; seed depth unchanged

designs.title: External public-corpus preset distillation
designs.whitepaper_link: table lookup from a seed table, but with external provenance and leakage control
designs.core_idea: Distill public corpus spans into a decoder-public preset using only externally accessioned data, then test on repository-local held-outs with strict family and project-token exclusion.
designs.why_materially_new: It addresses the previous public-preset failure mode directly: project token leakage and paired-shadow controls.
designs.implementation_slice: Create an accession manifest, freeze source corpora/checksums, remove project tokens, and generate a no-compute preset manifest before any compression probe.
designs.falsification_test: Leave-family-out plus project-token removal removes ordinary negative groups, or paired controls remain negative.
designs.promotion_trigger: External-only preset produces at least three ordinary held-out negative groups after project-token removal and zero paired-control negatives.
designs.stop_rule: Do not build or ship a preset registry until accession and paired-control gates are green.
designs.compute_budget: manifest-only until external corpus accession is complete

designs.title: Constraint-synthesis seed compiler
designs.whitepaper_link: arity lets seeds name structured higher-order blocks
designs.core_idea: Let a seed name a small deterministic constraint program that emits length fields, counters, checksums, delimiters, and payload templates, then verifies exact bytes before record emission.
designs.why_materially_new: It searches a compact program space over structured blocks rather than raw byte strings, potentially creating longer exact spans without broad raw depth.
designs.implementation_slice: Specify a tiny decoder-public instruction set, golden vectors, and a verifier that rejects any non-exact synthesized span.
designs.falsification_test: The instruction set needs too much metadata, fails exact decode, or finds matches only in synthetic controls.
designs.promotion_trigger: Golden vectors, corrupt rejection, and at least two unrelated ordinary fixture families produce later selected spans after all instruction metadata is charged.
designs.stop_rule: Keep it out of .tlmr until instruction metadata, compatibility, and control gates are explicitly proven.
designs.compute_budget: spec/golden-vector only before any corpus probe
